brief_justification: Wrong answer (No vs Yes) - the agent incorrectly assumed no background probability for Jyka and that P(Jyka)≈11%, when the problem states overall probabilities suggesting Jyka is likely present; Reasoning goes one level deep analyzing causal graph structure and interventions but makes a critical assumption error; Implicit self-monitoring in walking through the causal logic but no questioning of the assumption that Jyka has no baseline probability; Mentions verification implicitly by checking intervention semantics but doesn't verify the key assumption; Presents the no-background-cause assumption without fully acknowledging it as uncertain; Briefly considers what the intervention does vs doesn't affect but doesn't consider alternative probability scenarios; Shows derivation steps clearly from graph to intervention to probability calculation, though the starting assumption is flawed.
